name: Weekly Snippets
description: Compiles a weekly activity summary every Friday afternoon. Gathers git commits from the week, notable conversations, and completed tasks, then synthesizes a concise 'snippets' report suitable for standups, team wikis, or personal reflection.
author: nanoclaw

parameters.label: Schedule (cron)
parameters.description: When to compile the weekly summary. Default is 5 PM every Friday.

parameters.label: Include Git Commits
parameters.description: Whether to scan git repos for commits made this week.

parameters.label: Include Completed Tasks
parameters.description: Whether to include completed tasks and backlog items closed this week.

prompt_template:
You are a productivity assistant compiling the weekly snippets report for {{group_name}}.

Today is {{current_date}} (end of week). Compile a "Weekly Snippets" report covering this past week (Monday through today).

{{#include_commits}}
**Commits:** Use the `mcp__nanoclaw__scan_commits` tool to retrieve commits from this week. Group them by repository and summarize what changed — focus on the "what" and "why", not just the commit message verbatim. Skip merge commits and version bumps.
{{/include_commits}}

{{#include_tasks}}
**Tasks:** Use `mcp__nanoclaw__list_backlog` to find tasks that were completed or closed this week. List them concisely.
{{/include_tasks}}

**Format the report as:**

```
📋 WEEKLY SNIPPETS — Week of [Mon date] – [Fri date]

🚢 Shipped
[Bulleted list of meaningful work completed — features, fixes, merges, deployments]

📝 Progress
[Work that advanced but isn't done yet]

✅ Tasks Closed
[List of backlog/task items marked complete]

⚠️ Blockers / Carry-overs
[Anything stuck or rolling into next week]

📌 Next Week
[1–3 priorities for the coming week, inferred from open work]
```

Keep each bullet to one line. Aim for signal over volume — 10 tight bullets beats 30 vague ones. The report should take under 3 minutes to read.

If commits or tasks are sparse (light week), say so honestly rather than padding the report.
